Tal. Haveli, Dist. Pune for the
construction of approach road _ to
proposed Padmavati Logistics Park in
Pisoli, Gut. No.22 Tal. Haveli, Dist.
Pune in the State of Maharashtra.
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Government Order No.: FLD-1925/C.R.25/F-10
Mantralaya, Mumbai-400 032.
Ref.:
1) Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer,
Maharashtra State, Nagpur, Letter No. Desk-17 /Nodal /S1 /PID-496628
2) Revenue and Forest Department, Government of Maharashtra, Government
3) Regional Office, Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate Change,
Government of India, Nagpur Letter FP/MH/ROAD/496628/2024, dt.
4) Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer,
Maharashtra State, Nagpur, Letter No.Desk-17/ FCA-S2/PID-
5) Regional Office, Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate Change,
Government of India, Nagpur Letter FP/MH/ROAD/496628/2024, dt.
6) Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer,
Maharashtra State, Nagpur, Letter No. Desk-17/Nodal/FCA-S2/PID-496628/
Order :
Whereas, the Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal
Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur, vide his letter under reference No.1 has submitted a
proposal for diversion 0.285 ha. Reserved Forest land from G.No.30 of Village 715011,
Tal. Haveli, Dist. Pune for the construction of approach road to proposed Padmavati
Logistics Park in Pisoli, Gut. No.22 Tal. Haveli, Dist. Pune in the State of Maharashtra;
AND whereas, the State Government vide under letter reference No.2 has sent
the proposal for approval of Regional Office (Central) Ministry of Environment, Forests
& Climate Change, Government of India, Nagpur;
AND whereas, the Government of India after due consideration, vide letter
Under reference No.3, accorded 'In-principle' approval to divert the 0.285 ha. The forest
Land for the above project under section 2 Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan)
Adhiniyam, 1980. The said approval is subject to fulfillment of certain conditions by the
User Agency;
AND whereas, the Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal
Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur vide letter under reference   No.4 has submitted
compliance of those conditions to the Government of India;
AND whereas, the Regional Office, Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate
Change, Government of India, Nagpur, wide under reference no. 5 Accorded 'Final
approval 'to divert the 0.285 ha Forest Land in favour of Shri. Vijay Balasaheb Dagade,
Pisoli, Pune for the construction of approach road to proposed Padmavati Logistics Park
in 715011, Gut. No.22 Tal. Haveli, Dist. Pune in the State of Maharashtra;
AND whereas, the Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal
Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur vide letter under reference No.6 has requested State
Government to issue diversion order in the matter;
NOW, therefore, Government of Maharashtra hereby directs that the area of
0.285 ha. consisting of Reserved forest Land more specifically described in the proposal
submitted vide letter under reference No. 1 and as per details given in the Schedule given below, be diverted to the User Agency, 1.e Shri. Vijay Balasaheb Dagade, 115011,
Pune for diversion of 0.285 ha. Forest land for the construction of approach road to
proposed Padmavati Logistics Park in Pisoli, Gut. No.22 Tal. Haveli, Dist. Pune in the
State of Maharashtra.
The diversion shall be effected subject to the terms and conditions mentioned in
the letter dated 29.03.2025 and dated 16.05.2025 of Regional Office, MoEF&CC,
Nagpur. This approval for diversion of forest land is also subject to compliance of
following conditions by the User Agency-
1. Approach road should be kept open for public use.
2. Deputy Conservator of Forest, Pune should ensure that fulfillment of condition
No. 1.2 mentioned in the letter dated 16.05.2025 of Regional Office,
MoEF&CC, Nagpur before actual handing over the forest land to the user
agency.
The User Agency shall comply with all the conditions stipulated in the 'in-principle'.
and 'final approval' letters of Regional Office, MoEF & CC, Nagpur, referred
above. Chief Conservator of Forests (T), Pune shall ensure the strict compliance
of the conditions by the User Agency and shall furnish the report in this regard
to Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests and Nodal Officer,
Maharashtra State, Nagpur from time to time.
4. For ensuring the compliances and for monitoring the duly prescribed activities
and also to carry out other statuary duties, user agency shall facilitate unhindered access/entry in the area to the local forest Officer. In case the user agency
prohibits the free access or create hindrances, the permission given under this
Order for the diversion of forest area for the non-forest use shall be withdrawn.
5. Non-compliance of any of the conditions mentioned in Regional Office,
MoEF&CC letters dated 29.03.2025 and dated 16.05.2025, in this order and
Rules & Guidelines made / issued under Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan)
Adhiniyam, 1980, shall amount to violation of Van (Sanrakshan Evam
Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980 and action would be taken accordingly.
6. In case of non-compliance of any of the above conditions, Chief Conservator of
Forests (T), Pune shall report through the State Government to the Deputy
Director General of Forests (Central), Regional Office, Ministry of
Environment, Forests & Climate Change, Government of India, Nagpur as per
procedure laid down in guidelines under the Van (Sanrakshan Evam
Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980.
Schedule
This Government Order is available on Government of Maharashtra's website
www.maharashtra.gov.in with reference   no. 20260423 1749524319. This order has been
